notes: Total unique refs: 41. Summary: {'NAME_DRIFT_FLUTTER_RENAMED': 23, 'TRUE_FABRICATION_NOWHERE': 13, 'FLUTTER_GAP_OTHER_PLATFORMS_HAVE': 4, 'FLUTTER_EXTRACTOR_BLIND_SPOT': 1}. Key findings: (1) AmityPostRepository (×14) and AmityUserRepository (×4) = NAME_DRIFT — Flutter SDK uses PostRepository/UserRepository without Amity prefix, accessed via AmitySocialClient.newPostRepository() and AmityCoreClient.newUserRepository(). (2) AmityCommentReferenceType.post (×8), AmityImageSize.medium/small/full/large, AmityDataType.image/etc., AmityStreamStatus.LIVE/RECORDED/ENDED = NAME_DRIFT — Flutter SDK uses UPPER_SNAKE for DataType/ImageSize/CommentReferenceType enums and lowerCamelCase for StreamStatus. (3) AmityContentFlagReason.COMMUNITY_GUIDELINES and 8 other UPPER_SNAKE flag reasons = NAME_DRIFT — Flutter uses lowerCamelCase static const members. (4) AmityCoreClient.newClient (×6) = TRUE_FABRICATION — use setup() instead. (5) AmityCommentCreationData, AmityCommentUpdateData, AmityCommentQuery (TRUE_FABRICATION) — these are fabricated types used in comment creation/update flows. Flutter SDK uses builder patterns via repository methods directly.
